sn5.5:1.2: Then, early in the morning, Uppalavaṇṇā the nun adjusted her robes and, taking her bowl & outer robe, went into Sāvatthī for alms. When she had gone for alms in Sāvatthī and had returned from her alms round, after her meal she went to the Grove of the Blind to spend the day.
sn5.5:1.3: Having gone deep into the Grove of the Blind, she stood at the root of a flowering Sal tree.
sn5.5:1.4: Then Māra the Evil One, wanting to arouse fear, horripilation, & terror in her, wanting to make her fall away from concentration, approached her & addressed her in verse:
sn5.5:2.1: “You’ve come, nun, to this Sal tree
sn5.5:2.3: and stand alone at its root,
sn5.5:2.4: with no one to match you in beauty.
sn5.5:3.1: In your foolishness, aren’t you afraid of rape?”
sn5.5:3.2: Then the thought occurred to Uppalavaṇṇā the nun:
sn5.5:3.3: “Now who has recited this verse—a human being or a non-human one?” Then it occurred to her:
sn5.5:3.4: “This is Māra the Evil One who has recited this verse wanting to arouse fear, horripilation, & terror in me, wanting to make me fall away from concentration.”
sn5.5:3.5: Then, having understood that “This is Māra the Evil One,” she replied to him in verses:
sn5.5:4.3: I wouldn’t stir a hair. I’d feel no terror,
sn5.5:4.4: and I’m not afraid of you, Māra, even alone like this.
sn5.5:6.1: I have mastery over the mind,
sn5.5:6.2: have well-developed the bases of power.
sn5.5:6.4: and not afraid of you, my friend.”
sn5.5:7.1: Then Māra the Evil One—sad & dejected at realizing, “Uppalavaṇṇā the nun knows me”—vanished right there.
